title: When Reflecting on Your Past: - Write It Down: Keep a Journal of Key
subtitle: Spot Consistency Bias
description:
When reflecting on your past:
- Write it down: Keep a journal of key events and your thoughts at the time.
- Ask others: Check your memories against those who were present.
- Stay objective: Remind yourself that people change, and it’s okay if your past views differ from the present.

Example: If you recall always loving your current career, check past entries or conversations to see if you had doubts.

howTo.title: Create a Memory Snapshot
howTo.summary: Open the Brali LifeOS app and create a new Memory Snapshot. Include a short title, date, three verifiable facts, and two lines of your feelings with a confidence rating.

howTo.title: Attach Supporting Evidence
howTo.summary: If possible, attach a piece of evidence such as a screenshot, calendar invite, or chat excerpt to your Memory Snapshot. This will help verify your memories later.

howTo.title: Schedule a 7-Day Check-In
howTo.summary: Set a reminder for a 7-day check-in to review your Memory Snapshot. This will help you reflect on any changes in your thoughts or feelings about the event.

howTo.title: Reflect on Changes
howTo.summary: During your check-in, assess if new information has emerged and if your interpretation of the event has changed. Update your confidence rating and add any new insights.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app. Visit https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/consistency-bias-tracker/ to keep track of your progress.
